text: To manage a team effectively, communication is the foundation. Leaders should give clear instructions and also listen to their team members. Delegation is essential—assign tasks based on each person’s strengths so that work is balanced. Regular feedback helps people improve and feel valued. For example, praise good work and offer suggestions constructively. Motivation keeps the team engaged, whether through recognition or shared goals. However, conflicts may arise. Address them quickly by encouraging open discussion and finding fair solutions. By combining these skills, you can build a productive and supportive team.
